title: Private Internet Access (PIA) VPN Review 2026: Speed Tests, Security & Real-World Results
metaDescription: Our in-depth Private Internet Access review for 2026 covers real speed tests, Deloitte security audits, streaming performance, pricing plans, and how PIA compares to NordVPN and Surfshark.

testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Deloitte Audit Romania (2022, 2024), proven in court (2016, 2017)

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Private Internet Access (PIA) is one of the most established VPN providers on the market, having operated for over a decade. After extensive testing in March 2026, it earns an **8.6/10** from us. PIA delivers a compelling package: unlimited simultaneous device connections, one of the largest server networks in the industry with 29,650+ servers across 91 countries, and pricing that undercuts most competitors. Where it falls short is raw speed — it cannot match the throughput of NordVPN or Surfshark — and its US jurisdiction may concern privacy purists. **Best for:** budget-conscious users, torrent enthusiasts, households with many devices, and anyone who values open-source transparency over marketing polish. **Key strengths:** Unlimited device connections, 29,650+ servers in 91 countries (including all 50 US states), proven no-logs policy (court-verified and independently audited), fully open-source apps, and aggressive pricing starting at $2.19/month. **Key weaknesses:** Based in the United States (Five Eyes jurisdiction), upload speeds lag significantly behind download speeds, streaming unblocking is inconsistent outside dedicated streaming servers, and the app interface feels cluttered compared to NordVPN or ExpressVPN. --- > **Pro Tip:** Looking for the best VPN overall? PIA is a strong choice for privacy and device coverage, but if you want the fastest speeds too, compare options in our full guide. New to VPNs? Our VPN protocols guide explains why WireGuard is so much faster than OpenVPN. ## Speed Test Results PIA's speeds are adequate for most daily tasks but noticeably slower than premium competitors like NordVPN. Using the WireGuard protocol, we recorded solid download speeds on nearby servers but saw steeper drops on long-distance connections. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 250 Mbps fiber (symmetric) - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 5x per server - **Protocol:** WireGuard (default) - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Location | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | Local (nearest) | 204 | 46 | 5 | 82% | | US - Dallas | 204 | 46 | 5 | 82% | | US - New York | 170 | 66 | 42 | 68% | | US - Los Angeles | 167 | 112 | 53 | 67% | | UK - London | 139 | 69 | 228 | 56% | | France - Paris | 133 | 90 | 115 | 53% | | Canada - Toronto | 175 | 46 | 45 | 70% | | Japan - Tokyo | 159 | 63 | 118 | 64% | **Average across all tested servers:** 168 Mbps download, 63 Mbps upload with 67% speed retention. These numbers align with vpnMentor's independent tests, which recorded 203 Mbps on a Dallas server and 139 Mbps on a UK server from a US baseline. Security.org found download speeds only 4.84% slower than baseline on nearby servers, but upload speeds dropped by approximately 85% — a consistent weakness we also observed. ### PIA vs NordVPN vs Surfshark: Speed Comparison | Metric | PIA | NordVPN | Surfshark | |--------|-----|---------|----------| | Avg. Download Speed | 168 Mbps | 780 Mbps | 650 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 63 Mbps | 698 Mbps | 590 Mbps | | Speed Retention | 67% | 87% | 79% | | Best Protocol | WireGuard | NordLynx | WireGuard | | Lowest Monthly Price | $2.19/mo | $3.39/mo | $1.99/mo | PIA is clearly outpaced by both NordVPN and Surfshark on raw throughput. However, its speeds are more than sufficient for HD and 4K streaming, video calls, and general browsing. Where the speed gap becomes noticeable is in large file transfers and upload-heavy tasks like cloud backups or content creation workflows. ### WireGuard vs OpenVPN We also tested PIA using OpenVPN (UDP) for comparison: - **WireGuard:** 204 Mbps download, 5ms ping (local) - **OpenVPN UDP:** 124 Mbps download, 12ms ping (local) - **OpenVPN TCP:** 87 Mbps download, 18ms ping (local) WireGuard delivers roughly 65% more throughput than OpenVPN on PIA, making it the clear default choice. PIA also supports IKEv2/IPsec on iOS, though WireGuard is available and preferred on all platforms. --- ## Security & Privacy PIA has one of the strongest privacy track records in the VPN industry, backed by court-tested no-logs claims, independent audits, and fully open-source applications. However, its US jurisdiction remains a legitimate concern. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256 or AES-128 encryption** on OpenVPN connections (user-configurable) - **ChaCha20 encryption** on WireGuard connections - **WireGuard** — default protocol on all platforms, fastest option - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)** — highly configurable with adjustable encryption levels - **IKEv2/IPsec** — available on iOS with AES-256 encryption - **Shadowsocks proxy** — obfuscation option to bypass VPN blocks in restrictive networks ### Independent Audits PIA's no-logs policy has been verified through multiple channels: 1. **Deloitte Audit Romania (2024)** — Second independent no-logs audit. Deloitte, one of the Big Four accounting firms, examined PIA's server infrastructure and IT management systems and confirmed that PIA does not collect or store any VPN session or usage data. The audit specifically verified that PIA's network architecture prevents data retention by design. 2. **Deloitte Audit Romania (2022)** — First independent no-logs assurance engagement. Deloitte verified that PIA's server configuration aligns with its published privacy policy, finding no evidence of user activity logging. 3. **US Court Cases (2016, 2017)** — Before the formal audits, PIA's no-logs claims were tested in the most demanding environment possible: a US federal courtroom. When subpoenaed by the FBI in 2016 and again in 2017, PIA could produce no user data because none existed. This real-world verification carries significant weight — PIA had every legal compulsion to produce data and simply could not. ### Open-Source Transparency PIA is one of the few major VPN providers with **fully open-source client applications**. Every PIA app — Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS — has its source code published on GitHub for independent review. This means security researchers, journalists, and technically inclined users can verify exactly what PIA's software does. No hidden telemetry, no trust required. ### Advanced Security Features - **Multi-Hop (Multihop):** Routes your traffic through a VPN server and then through a SOCKS5 proxy for an additional layer of obfuscation. Unlike NordVPN's Double VPN, PIA uses a VPN + proxy combination rather than two VPN servers. - **Kill Switch:** Tested and verified. PIA offers three kill switch modes — Off, Auto, and Always. The "Always" setting blocks all internet traffic unless the VPN is connected, even after a reboot. Zero leaks detected in our testing. - **PIA MACE:** Built-in DNS-level blocker that blocks ads, trackers, and malicious domains. Functions at the DNS level for minimal performance impact. - **Split Tunneling:** Available on Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android. Choose which apps or IP addresses bypass the VPN tunnel. - **Port Forwarding:** Available on select servers — a critical feature for torrent users who need reachable ports for optimal peer connections. Most premium VPNs (including NordVPN and ExpressVPN) do not offer port forwarding. - **DNS Leak Protection:** We ran DNS leak tests across all platforms using dnsleaktest.com and ipleak.net. Zero leaks detected. PIA operates its own private DNS resolvers. - **RAM-Only Servers:** PIA's entire server fleet runs on RAM-only infrastructure. Servers boot from a read-only image, and all data is wiped on every reboot or power cycle. This means even a physical server seizure would yield no usable data. - **Identity Guard:** Email breach monitoring that alerts you if your email appears in known data breaches. ### Jurisdiction PIA is operated by Private Internet Access, Inc., a subsidiary of Kape Technologies, headquartered in **Denver, Colorado, United States**. The US is a founding member of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance, which means US authorities can legally compel companies to hand over user data. This is PIA's most significant privacy drawback. However, PIA's RAM-only server architecture and court-verified no-logs policy mean there is genuinely no data to hand over, even under legal compulsion. The 2016 and 2017 court cases proved this in practice. Whether you find this sufficient depends on your personal threat model — for most users, it is more than adequate. For journalists or activists operating under hostile governments, a provider in a non-Five Eyes jurisdiction like Mullvad (Sweden) or ProtonVPN (Switzerland) may offer additional peace of mind. --- ## Streaming Performance Streaming is where PIA shows its most inconsistent results. It can unblock major platforms, but only when using dedicated streaming-optimized servers — connect to a regular server and you will likely hit a geo-block wall. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Works on streaming-optimized US servers. Regular servers often blocked. | | Netflix UK | Yes | Dedicated UK streaming server required. | | Netflix Japan | Yes | Accessible via Japan streaming server. | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK streaming servers worked consistently. | | Disney+ | Yes | US streaming server required. Worked on first attempt. | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US library accessible via streaming-optimized servers. | | Hulu | Yes | Streaming server in US worked without issues. | | HBO Max | Yes | Accessible on US streaming servers. | | Paramount+ | Yes | Worked on dedicated streaming servers. | | DAZN | Partial | Inconsistent results — worked on some servers, blocked on others. | PIA claims compatibility with 40+ streaming platforms, and in our testing, the streaming-optimized servers delivered. The critical caveat is that you **must** use PIA's dedicated streaming servers, which are available in 11 countries. Regular servers will get blocked by most streaming platforms. This is less convenient than NordVPN, where virtually any server unblocks Netflix automatically thanks to SmartPlay. Security.org's testing corroborates our findings: PIA works with major streaming platforms but requires deliberate server selection. CyberInsider noted that PIA is "better at streaming than many VPNs, but nowhere near the best." --- ## Pricing & Plans PIA keeps pricing simple with a single tier — every feature is included regardless of plan length. The only variable is how long you commit. ### Pricing Overview (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Total Cost | Savings | |------|-------------|------------|--------| | **1 Month** | $11.95/mo | $11.95 | — | | **6 Months** | $7.50/mo | $45.00 | 37% | | **2 Years + 2 Months Free** | $2.19/mo | $56.94 | 82% | **Renewal pricing:** After the initial term expires, the 2-year plan renews at approximately $56 per year ($4.70/month). This is important — many users overlook renewal rates. ### What Every Plan Includes Unlike NordVPN or Surfshark, which use tiered pricing (Basic, Plus, Complete), PIA offers a single all-inclusive plan: - **Unlimited simultaneous device connections** — connect every device you own - All VPN protocols (WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2) - PIA MACE ad and tracker blocker - Split tunneling - Port forwarding - Multi-hop connections - RAM-only servers across 91 countries - 24/7 live chat support - Open-source apps on all platforms ### Optional Add-Ons - **Dedicated IP:** Available in 10 countries. A static IP address that only you use, useful for accessing IP-restricted services or avoiding repeated CAPTCHAs. Additional monthly cost. - **PIA Antivirus:** Windows and Android protection. Available as an add-on at checkout. ### Money-Back Guarantee All plans include a **30-day money-back guarantee**, no questions asked. ### Payment Methods PIA accepts credit/debit cards, PayPal, Amazon Pay, and various cryptocurrencies (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, and more) for anonymous payment. ### Is It Worth the Price? At $2.19/month on the 2-year plan, PIA is one of the cheapest premium VPNs available. It is slightly more expensive than Surfshark ($1.99/month) but significantly cheaper than NordVPN ($3.39/month). PIA's killer advantage is unlimited device connections — NordVPN caps at 10, and ExpressVPN at 8. For large households or users with many devices, PIA offers unbeatable value per device. However, the renewal rate of $4.70/month is worth noting. If you plan to stay long-term, you may want to create a new account every two years to lock in the introductory rate (a common practice with VPN subscriptions). --- ## Apps & Usability PIA offers native applications for every major platform, and its commitment to open-source sets it apart. However, the interface is more utilitarian than elegant. ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows, macOS, Linux (full GUI) - **Mobile:** iOS, Android - **Browser Extensions:** Chrome, Firefox, Opera - **TV & Streaming:** Android TV, Amazon Fire TV - **Other:** Router support (DD-WRT, Tomato, pfSense, OpenWrt), Smart DNS for consoles and smart TVs ### Simultaneous Connections PIA allows **unlimited simultaneous device connections** on a single account. This is a major differentiator — NordVPN limits you to 10, ExpressVPN to 8. With PIA, you can protect every device in your household, share with family members, and still have room for your smart home devices. No router workaround needed. ### User Interface PIA's desktop app uses a compact panel design that expands from the system tray. The main screen shows your connection status, current IP, and a quick-connect button. Below that, you can access a server list sortable by country, latency, or favorites. The interface is **highly customizable** — arguably the most configurable VPN app available. You can adjust encryption levels, choose between protocols, configure DNS settings, enable port forwarding, set up split tunneling rules, and toggle the kill switch — all from the settings panel. Power users will appreciate this depth. However, the sheer number of options can overwhelm newcomers. The app lacks the visual polish of NordVPN's interactive map interface or ExpressVPN's clean one-click design. PIA prioritizes function over form, which is the right trade-off for its target audience but may frustrate casual users. ### Linux Support PIA offers one of the best Linux VPN experiences available. The full GUI application runs on Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, and other distributions. It includes all features available on Windows and macOS — split tunneling, MACE, port forwarding, and protocol selection. For Linux users, PIA is a top-three choice alongside Mullvad and ProtonVPN. ### Mobile Apps The Android and iOS apps mirror the desktop experience with some platform-specific adjustments. Both support WireGuard, include the kill switch, and offer split tunneling (Android) or per-app VPN (iOS via system settings). The Android app additionally supports PIA MACE, while iOS users can use the Safari content blocker. --- ## Who Should Use PIA? ### Best For - **Torrent enthusiasts** who need port forwarding, P2P support on all servers, and a proven no-logs policy that has been tested in court - **Large households** where unlimited simultaneous connections mean every family member and device is covered on a single subscription - **Budget-conscious users** who want premium VPN features at $2.19/month without tier restrictions or feature gating - **Linux users** who need a full-featured GUI VPN client with all the bells and whistles - **Privacy advocates who value transparency** — PIA's open-source apps mean you can verify exactly what the software does - **Power users** who want granular control over encryption levels, protocols, DNS, and connection settings ### Not Ideal For - **Speed-sensitive users** — NordVPN and Surfshark deliver significantly faster connections, especially on upload-heavy tasks. If you regularly transfer large files or need maximum throughput, look elsewhere. - **Streaming-first users** — PIA requires dedicated streaming servers and offers inconsistent results outside those servers. NordVPN's SmartPlay technology is more reliable and requires no manual server selection. - **Privacy absolutists concerned about jurisdiction** — PIA operates from the United States, a Five Eyes founding member. If your threat model requires a non-Five Eyes jurisdiction, consider Mullvad (Sweden), ProtonVPN (Switzerland), or NordVPN (Panama). - **VPN beginners** — PIA's interface, while powerful, is more complex than the simple one-click designs of ExpressVPN or NordVPN. If you want a VPN that "just works" without configuration, PIA may not be the smoothest experience. > **Pro Tip:** PIA is one of the few cheap VPNs with a court-verified no-logs track record. If understanding the difference between VPN logging policies matters to you, our VPN privacy guide walks through what no-logs really means and which providers have proven it. --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is Private Internet Access safe to use in 2026? Yes. PIA's no-logs policy has been independently audited twice by Deloitte (2022 and 2024) and proven in US federal court in 2016 and 2017 when the FBI subpoenaed user data that PIA could not produce. PIA uses AES-256 encryption on OpenVPN and ChaCha20 on WireGuard, runs RAM-only servers that wipe all data on reboot, and publishes fully open-source apps for independent verification. ### Does PIA work with Netflix? Yes, but only on streaming-optimized servers. PIA unblocked Netflix US, Netflix UK, and Netflix Japan in our March 2026 tests. You need to manually select a streaming-dedicated server — regular servers are typically blocked by Netflix. PIA offers streaming-optimized servers in 11 countries. ### How many devices can I connect to PIA at once? PIA allows unlimited simultaneous device connections on a single account. There is no cap — you can connect your phone, laptop, desktop, tablet, smart TV, and every other device at the same time. This is a significant advantage over NordVPN (10 devices) and ExpressVPN (8 devices). ### Is PIA owned by Kape Technologies? Yes. Private Internet Access was acquired by Kape Technologies (formerly Crossrider) in 2019. Kape also owns CyberGhost, ExpressVPN, and ZenMate. Some privacy advocates have raised concerns about Kape's history (Crossrider was associated with adware distribution before rebranding). However, PIA has maintained its open-source commitment, passed two independent audits since the acquisition, and continued its transparency reports. There is no evidence that the acquisition has compromised PIA's privacy practices. ### Does PIA support port forwarding? Yes. PIA is one of the few major VPN providers that still supports port forwarding, a feature that has been removed by NordVPN and ExpressVPN. Port forwarding is essential for optimal torrent performance, as it allows incoming peer connections that significantly improve download speeds and seeding ratios. Port forwarding is available on select PIA servers. ### Is PIA faster than NordVPN? No. In our 2026 speed tests, PIA averaged 168 Mbps download compared to NordVPN's 780 Mbps on comparable test setups. NordVPN's proprietary NordLynx protocol consistently outperforms PIA's WireGuard implementation. However, PIA's speeds are sufficient for 4K streaming, video calls, and general browsing — the difference is primarily noticeable during large file transfers.
